frameworks.name: computational electric field modeling (SimNIBS)
frameworks.one_line: Finite-element simulation of tES-induced electric fields in individual head models

frameworks.name: linear mixed-effects modeling
frameworks.one_line: Statistical attribution of E-field variability to anatomical predictors

core_question: Which head and brain anatomical features drive inter-individual variability of tES-induced electric fields, and does their impact differ between young and older adults?
key_assumptions: SimNIBS-modelled fields approximate true in-brain E-fields
Extra- and intra-cranial tissue volumes index anatomical variability
Conventional electrode montages are representative of tES practice
manipulations: Tissue compartment volumes (head, skin, skull, CSF, brain)
key_findings: Mixed models explained most inter-individual variability in overall E-field strength but not in focality
Higher head volume and relative skin, skull, and CSF volumes were associated with lower E-field strength
Age group interacted with head volume and CSF, with the volume-field relationship mitigated in older adults
interpretations: Global head anatomy is a major source of tES dose variability, and its influence is age-dependent
Anatomical variability contributes to inconsistent tES behavioral and neurophysiological outcomes
limitations_noted: Focality variance remained largely unexplained by the modeled anatomical predictors
